channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: A look at how federal agencies rang in the new year
item.description: With the sky filled with fireworks, the Washington Monument is illuminated as a candle as part of the 250th anniversary celebration for the United States, Thursday, Jan. 1, 2026, in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Amber Corrin, Federal News Network

item.title: Unannounced 32TB Seagate hard drive surfaces in Japan for photoshoot
item.description: Seagate’s unannounced 32TB IronWolf Pro hard drive surfaced in Japanese retail, marking a quiet shift of extreme capacity toward consumers.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: How to watch NHL Winter Classic 2026: live stream New York Rangers vs Florida Panthers, TV channels, preview
item.description: Here's how to watch Rangers vs Panthers live streams online in the 2026 NHL Winter Classic, as the traditional New Year's game heads to Florida for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Adam Marshall, TechRadar

item.title: The 14 global trends that will shape the climate in 2026
item.description: The future of the energy transition is being shaped by President Donald Trump’s climate policy rollbacks that favor fossil fuels — but also the life support for renewables and batteries paradoxically coming from data center power demand.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: AI may not be the federal buzzword for 2026
item.description: Federal News Network asked federal acquisition and IT experts what initiatives they are paying close attention to in the new year. The post AI may not be the federal buzzword for 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026
item.description: The White House is expected to kick off 2026 with a new national cyber strategy. Here's what else we're watching in a consequential year for cyber. The post Five things to watch in cybersecurity for 2026 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: AI disclosure in healthcare: What patients must know
item.description: States require AI disclosure in healthcare as California, Colorado and Utah implement laws mandating transparency when artificial intelligence influences patient care decisions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Experts warn PC prices could rise even more in 2026 as RAM costs set to soar higher
item.description: PCs and smartphones could become far more expensive from the second half of 2026 due to memory shortages, experts warn.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: Cloud costs now No. 2 expense at midsize IT companies behind labor
item.description: Midsize IT companies now spend huge percentages of their revenues on cloud services, driven by a growing number of AI workloads running in the cloud, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Back to the future: The most popular JavaScript stories and themes of 2025
item.description: Artificial intelligence and its promise to revolutionize programming—and possibly overthrow human sovereignty—is a central story of the post-Covid world. But for JavaScript developers, it is only one of the forces contending for center stage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: For Apple in ’26, it’s time to take the ‘Snow Leopard’ approach to OS updates
item.description: If the current speculation is correct, Apple’s 2026 operating system releases will follow a familiar path: they’re more likely to mimic the macOS Snow Leopard pattern set in 2009 than deliver a raft of snazzy new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to unlock the power of ChatGPT
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. I’ve recently written about free, private AI tools and the best AI mobile apps.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: NIH wants 1 million Americans for a study
item.description: "We don't normally rank our findings, but access controls tend to drift to the top because that is the gate to the system," Charles Summers said. The post NIH wants 1 million Americans for a study — but first must close critical data security gaps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta buys Manus for $2 billion to power high-stakes AI agent race
item.description: Meta’s $2 billion acquisition of Manus signals a major shift toward AI agents that can autonomously handle real work, not just generate responses.
item.dc:creator: Eric Hal Schwartz, TechRadar

item.title: Wispr CEO: What a post-keyboard office might look like
item.description: Voice-to-text tools have been around for decades, yet there’s little sign that office workers are relying any less on their keyboards.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From integration pain to partnership gain: How collaboration strengthens cybersecurity
item.description: There are several data-related barriers that can limit collaboration gains. Unsurprisingly, and with good reason, fear of inadvertently compromising systems stands at the top of the list, especially when external partners are involved.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google may soon let users change their Gmail address
item.description: Google may be about to let users change their Gmail address. According to Telegram group Google Pixel Hub, via 9to5Google, a Hindi version of Gmail’s support page states that the feature is “being rolled out gradually to all users”.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta buys high-profile AI startup Manus
item.description: Meta is now buying the hyped AI startup Manus for about $2 billion, TechCrunch reported. Founded in Singapore by former Chinese entrepreneurs, Manus gained widespread attention after a viral demo video showing an AI agent that could handle everything from job interviews to stock analysis.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 2026: The year we stop trusting any single cloud
item.description: For more than a decade, many considered cloud outages a theoretical risk, something to address on a whiteboard and then quietly deprioritize during cost cuts. In 2025, this risk became real.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What's inside Mexico's Popocatépetl? Scientists obtain first 3D images of the whole volcano
item.description: Scientists from Mexico’s National Autonomous University have created the first three-dimensional image of the Popocatépetl volcano's interior.
item.dc:creator: María Verza, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 4 New Year’s resolutions for devops success
item.description: It has been a dramatic and challenging year for developers and engineers working in devops organizations. More companies are using AI and automation for both development and IT operations, including for writing requirements, maintaining documentation, and vibe coding.
item.dc:creator: Info World
